lines.text: You've come, warrior!
The mighty guardian, our Iudicis Arsinosa, is not the kind of Echo to warm to crowds.
The Lioness favors the strong, the unyielding, and the battle-hardened. Only those she deems worthy will find her arms welcoming.
But... she is still an Echo and can't talk.
What is it? Did you feel something just now?
The <te href=850160>Vale of Glory</te>.
It's where all heroes, blessed by the glory forged in iron and fire, find their rest.
But on the long path of hunting, many died with their bodies missing. Only their weapons returned.
Arsinosa's frequency is woven from the cries of souls in battle. The battered weapons still carry the will of their former masters, whispering the deeds left undone.
To touch such weapons is a rite of great importance for those who claim glory in the Great Agon and receive the Lioness's blessing.
Hearing the voices of our ancestors by Arsinosa's side is a great source of strength for Septimontians.
She waits for us there. Shall we go together?
I'm not sure if Septimontian rituals will interest you, but you might uncover something useful, no?
Perfect! Let's head out.
Ugh...
Nothing... just...
...Just a dizzy spell. Overexcitement after the win... or something.
Anyway, it's no matter. Let's continue.
Hmm? Hey, is something wrong?
Is there something you need? Or... something you want to say?
I'm sorry. I don't recall crossing paths with Cartethyia. But... you could be right. I may have, and just forgotten about it.
These past days, there have been moments when... I feel lost, forgetting what I was doing a heartbeat ago.
I don't know if it's the Threnodian's work, but... if something is wrong, and it's trying to twist my mind...
You'd make the right call. Wouldn't you, my fellow warrior?
You'd protect my honor and my dignity.
Heh, well then, that'll do just fine!
I trust you, warrior, just as you trust me.
...Is that so?
I don't know what's weighing on your mind, but you accepted my invitation, and we fought as one in the arena.
As a warrior, I trust the clash of steel far more than words!
The traces of the blade reflect the soul of its wielder.
Alright! Don't wear that face. Whatever it is, I trust you'll make the right call.
Septimont is my city. Don't let it lose its glory or pride. Will you promise me that, warrior?
The Tidal Blight... It's an outgrowth of the Dark Tide itself.
Why is it appearing suddenly like this...?
What's that sound... Come, we should check it out!
This... doesn't feel like the first time I've been here.
Let's go.
Ha... huff... Ugh!
A-a dream? No, it couldn't...
It's no dream.
I'm not alone. The Warrior put the hope of change in my hands.
Heh... This feels good. Not standing there alone on the champion's stage.
({PlayerName} hasn't learned this yet, but I now remember traveling with Cartethyia. I can't be in the real world.)
(This is a Sonoro created by the Fractsidus. It's a realm of <color=RedD>stories</color>.)
(The story of the Great Agon lasts only three days. Before Cartethyia came here, I... had wandered this same bloody story more times than I can count.)
(The Dark Tide consumes Septimont, and that monstrous half-human, half-sea creature grows ever larger... Is this the Fractsidus plan? To feed it with Septimont's ruins?)
(A script... Yes, this Sonoro is like a book of plays.)
(Each world in the Sonoro is a story that keeps repeating, like sheets of paper, all <color=RedD>overlapping</color> with one another.)
(New pages keep appearing, layering over the old, and the changes in one story bleed through to the next.)
(After Cartethyia's actions, Septimont, once destroyed by the Dark Tide, has begun to return to normal in the story where {PlayerName} arrived.)
(Cartethyia saved me, changed my fate, and led me to the Warrior, who stopped the Threnodian in the last story. So in this time, I haven't lost my memory.)
({PlayerName} had delivered a powerful blow against the monster, and the energy supply from the Central Hub has been cut off.)
(But that's not even its full form...)
(I once searched for its true form with Cartethyia. I remember now, what we seek lies in the lower city's waters.)
I won't let your trust be misplaced, {PlayerName}.
(I've always carried this shame.)
(Shame for something I didn't choose, shame for something that's just there. Sometimes, it turns into doubt.）
(I try to live free! And I do, most days. But still...  I see it. I see the pain in their faces.)
(In the ones who fall behind. The ones who leave the field beaten. The ones who pin all their hopes on me...)
(I've always wondered, if I didn't have this gift, could I... could I've reached this point with clean hands?)
(Should I have judged those who trample the rules to survive? Was I blind to my own arrogance?)
Now... I don't think like that anymore.
{PlayerName} is stronger than me, no doubt, but {Male=he;Female=she} treats everyone the same. {Male=He;Female=She} didn't choose to stand alone. Instead, {Male=he;Female=she} chose to trust me.
Cartethyia spent years trapped by the weight of everyone's expectations. Never stepped foot in Septimont before, but still sailed across the seas to be here.
And Mya... Her Resonance ability might be weak, but she never ran from a fight. She fought 'til her last breath.
A heart isn't shaped by what it's born with or what it's been given.
I've underestimated others... and I've underestimated myself, too.
But you! Arrogance! Thinking you understand the darkness in people's hearts. But you don't have a single drop of life in you.
Beautiful as ever, the <te href=850150>Gryphon's Fortress</te>. My city, Septimont.
Built by the hands of the people. Not the Sentinel, and not the Threnodian.
Where there's light, there's always shadow.
But people need light. They'll chase it no matter what. And they'll learn to live with the shadow.
Mya, Cartethyia… They were born and raised on these islands. They are daughters of Rinascita. They belong to no gods but their own!
It's real… Both of you are safe!
Heh, you're lively. Your Echo's more boisterous than a Ta-da!
It's Mya's sword. I went through a lot to bring it back.
The monster is gone now. Like so many Septimontian heroes whose bodies were never recovered, I... wanted to return her weapon.
This is where she gave her life. I can't say if she loved or hated Septimont more.
But... she once spoke of the Bloodleaf Viburnum. It's a tribute often given to Gladiators by their supporters.
So, I think... she would have wanted to rest here.
It's beautiful...
Our city, the City of Glory. The people here love to drink, laugh, carve statues, and hunt.
It burns like fire, drenched in both victory and defeat. It holds all our disappointments and all our hopes.
That direction... It leads to the hunting grounds.
The <te href=850171>Blightcloud</te> still lingers there. When the <te href=850159>High Tide</te> comes, Septimontians hunt and cull the spawning Tacet Discords.
Of course! It would be my honor.
But before we go, there's something I need to do first.
Is that... Contender Mya?
Tell me what I need to do.
(In a past <color=RedD>story</color>, I came here with Cartethyia...)
(I remember the entrance to the underground waterway is in the Old Districts Bell Tower.)
(Ah... Of course! The upkeep of these waters always falls under the Silvas' jurisdiction.)
(Maybe I can awaken this sleeping Giant of Glory.)
(In Septimontian tradition, when it bears its blade and watches over the warriors leaving for the hunt, all gates and passages open.)
(Many of the old routes are blocked, but I can try draining the water through the remaining ones. It's worth a try!)
The submerged aqueducts... I remember reading about them in the Silva records. There used to be an underground waterway leading to the plains.
But now, with the Old District completely flooded, we can only access it if we drain the water first.
(After my role was erased, Cartethyia must've found the half-human, half-sea creature... and the Fractsidus.)
(Like {PlayerName}, she chose to remain on the closing of that page. To give new stories a fighting chance.)
(That's why... I only regained my name after I met the Warrior.)
(During our journey, the overlapping worlds the Warrior saw were the monster's doing. It tried to use Mya to steal my role again... to corrupt Septimont once more.)
(Only I can erase it from this story. Only I can prove that I'm real, and it is fake.)
...What happened to the Mya who vanished in the Vale of Glory decades ago?
After everything you went through, what did the city of Septimont mean to you...
Reconsider? Ha! You think I should keep playing along and stay blind to your match-fixing?
I don't care what you do off the field, but in the arena, fairness isn't negotiable. If you can't get that, just keep wagging your tail for the house...
You know, I once thought you were a decent Gladiator, and we fought well together. But look at you now. Pathetic.
Sorry about that clown show. Don't judge Septimont by a few rats that can't hold their own in the dirt. They're not the norm here.
Lupa's the name. <te href=850164>Gladiator</te> by trade. Guess we're acquainted now after crossing blades. Care to tell me your name?
Got it. It's rare to meet someone with your kind of strength.
Combat makes the greatest warriors. You're a fine example of that.
Ah, so you like hunting Tacet Discords? Then you've come to the right place.
I'm used to fighting the Gladiators here, but the way you handled your blade... it's beautiful. I like it.
Heh... just about. That man, Emilio, used to be my partner. <te href=850169>The Silvas</te> were probably behind this.
The House of Silva is nobility in Septimont. I share their name, though, I'm sure they've thrown me out since I stepped away from the team.
Yes. I knew something was off, but I didn't think they'd go this far. Maybe they threw some serious money at it.
I thought the money I made them would've been enough. Oh well!
I was thinking, you're not a local, are you? How about—
We team up and fight together in the finals?
Huh? {PlayerName}, is that your way of saying yes?
Cartethyia... No, never heard of her. What's going on with her?
Arsinosa is the symbol of our spirit, the great Echo guarding Septimont for centuries. Besides the current <te href=850157>Ephor</te>, Augusta, nobody has ever faced her directly.
I don't know if this Cartethyia of yours is strong enough to take on Arsinosa. If she did, it would be the talk of Septimont.
Well, that's why you should join me in the Agon. When Arsinosa shows up in the finals, you might find the answers you're looking for.
The champion gets noble recognition, a hefty bonus, and some secret perks. If we win, you can take it all.
If that doesn't interest you, then the attention that comes with being a champion will help you find the information you need on her.
Me? I just want to see the look on their cheating faces when they realize I've beaten them at their own game!
They're probably betting against me in secret. Ha, doesn't matter. Once we win, they'll lose it all. Serves 'em right.
If there's no punishment for crossing the line, then what's the point of a line at all?
I don't know what other tricks my dear family has planned, but you? You're an outlander. Strong, too. No ties to their schemes.
That's why I look forward to walking this path with you. Can't wait to see what kind of Gladiator you'll be!
That's the spirit! Well then...  I'll be counting on you, my fellow warrior.
Now, let's go register our <te href=850155>team</te>!
We did it!
That was perfection! Haven't felt this alive in ages.
I could finally focus and savor every moment of the fight…
Thanks, warrior. Teaming up with you was the right choice.
Ta-das. These little ones love a good match and never miss a chance to cheer for the victors.
They're more fired up than usual. Looks like your performance really won them over, {PlayerName}.
This city does know how to keep spirits high! Speaking of Gryphons, they've actually been here long before our ancestors even settled on this archipelago.
Anyway, we've secured our first victory. The next match will be held further out.
If we take down the top-ranked team there, we'll be… Huh?
I'm sorry. It's been too long since I've fought outside the Colosseum. Should've worn a helmet.
It comes with the job. Gotta draw a crowd in, both in and out of the arena.
Don't worry! I'm confident we'll claim the title. Soon enough, you'll be signing autographs 'til your hand bleeds.
Alright, everyone…
Uh... I-I can handle this, but it'll take too long.
We need to make a swift retreat.
Easy. Just hold my hand and don't let go!
Oh, me? I just... sent word to certain people.
Too much modesty is a double-edged sword, so I'll be frank. When it comes to bankability, I'm at the top of the game.
That's how my family, the Silvas, have kept their position all these years. But the nobles here in Septimont are always hunting and contesting for more.
Lions and wolves tear each other to pieces, while the hyenas lie in wait, eyes on their bellies. Everyone wants a taste of the juiciest meat.
And with the illegal betting they're running... Once they hear I've left the old team, they'll all be scrambling to get a piece of the action.
The waters are already so murky no one can tell who's stirring them.
But it's still better now than it was before. The mess they made before the current Ephor took over was...
Ha... I see you've been waiting for me, Cartur.
He was my manager back in the Bough of Legions. Has a gift for knowing exactly what to say to anyone, though that's his flaw, too.
And? What's your point?
With me and {PlayerName}? One hundred percent.
Enough, Cartur. Spare me the games and empty chatter! I have one question for you...
...Are you involved in the Silvas' match-fixing?
Fair enough. You may leave now.
I know the rules well enough.
...I just wonder if Domina Julia truly understands who's making the rules now, and where she stands in this game.
Let's go, my fellow warrior.
It's all going as planned! Let's get you settled somewhere comfortable, then tomorrow, we head to the Colosseum Olymdos for the semi-finals.
A body of water? Yes, but it's been abandoned for years. That elevator can take you to the upper city, though.
If you want to try looking for her, we can start there!
Yes?
I didn't have time to gather enough proof before I left. Besides, this isn't the right moment.
Once we win the Great Agon, I'll make everything public. In Septimont, only the winners get to speak their truth.
Hehe, I'm fine. High praise leads to harsh words. It's always like that.
Heh, a strong warrior with a kind heart... Rare traits for a hunter.
Honestly, I don't care what people say. This kind of thing happens every day in Septimont. If you can't handle it, you won't last.
Tarnishing rivals' reputations is one of their usual tricks. I cut off their betting profits, so it's only natural they'd be upset.
Besides, now I have a reliable warrior by my side. That's all that matters.
No worrying about whether my teammate can keep up, no dealing with petty politics... just the thrill of the fight.
Thank you for that, {PlayerName}.
How?
Alright then. I'll wait here. Good luck!
How's it going? You look... frustrated.
Don't worry, we can follow the lead on Arsinosa.
That's right! I'm sure you'll make progress by the finals.
Tercules… His statues once stood tall on both sides of the street, watching over the heroes of Septimont as they set out to hunt.
Back then, the chimes drifting up from the city were the finest sounds of glory.
Welcome to my city, Gryphon's Fortress of Capitoline Hill!
Oh, some minor noble houses that didn't make it into the Agon.
But in Septimont, strength and power go hand in hand. Nobles get more, but they better prove they're worth it and pull their weight for the city.
The Agon isn't just about fortune. It's a way to find the capable. For nobles, the ability to command and control Echoes is a basic responsibility.
If the heir of a noble house isn't strong enough, it signals that the house is unfit to hunt down Tacet Discords or protect Septimont.
So... if a noble's been living it up, they'll end up broke and thrown out of the city after paying high patronage fees for a gladiator who doesn't even make the cut.
"Strength is everything." That's how it works. You've fought your battles too, or you wouldn't be here.
Might not work like this everywhere, but here in Septimont, with the Dark Tide breathing down our necks, it's our survival code.
For most Septimontians, the Agon isn't just an event. It's the training ground nobles and commoners alike have to fight in.
The victors show no mercy to the defeated. People here despise weakness, worship strength, and crave glory.
Where you come from don't matter. Prove yourself, and the crowd will scream your name. The spotlight's yours, the arena's your stage.
But if you can't can't keep up, if you're not chosen... you'll be left in the shadows.
Hi there. Is team registration still open?
Plans changed. I'm no longer with the Bough of Legions. This is my new partner, {PlayerName}.
Hmm… {PlayerName}, why don't you do the honors?
We Septimontians live alongside the waves, and I've always found their sound soothing. A perfect choice!
Haha, I take it you are referring to the two of us. The star does suit you well.
Haha, simple but powerful! A good omen for us, too.
Alright, the name is settled. We'd also like to apply for the special qualifier, please.
The <te href=850165>Gladiator's Agon</te> happens year-round in Septimont. Training matches, exhibition games, open tournaments, you name it.
But we're after the Agon of Champions, the biggest event held every four years. We also call it the Great Agon.
There are two stages: a points-based prelim and an elimination round. The prelim is almost over. The eliminations are about to begin.
I've earned enough points from the regular matches to qualify for the finals, but since you're a new contender, you'll need to win the special qualifier to make it.
If we defeat higher-ranked opponents, we can secure a spot with fewer matches.
Exactly. It's one of the reforms the new Ephor made to the Agon. And of course, our Echo partners will be joining us in the arena too!
Echoes aren't just companions to us, but proof of our strength. Hunting them, taming them, and learning to live with them is the first lesson every Septimontian learns.
Now, it's time to step into the arena. Ready?
Let's see how well we work together in the arena, my fellow warrior.
Fulvia! Two large platters please, with two bottles of <te href=850151>Heartpour</te>!
Still got that fire, huh, Fulvia?
Haha… well, back then a strong backer was exactly what I needed. Can't say it was all bad.
What? Oh, come on...
A drink I endorsed. Most of the drinks I promoted were... Ahem. But this one's actually really good.
If my name's on it, you don't have to worry about quality. It's just that the taste... well, let's just say it's "meh" at best.
Anyway, I'll get you a taste of Heartpour later. For now, let's enjoy the meal!
So, fellow warrior, any stories to share? There is still much to learn about each other.
Oh thanks! I almost forgot with everything going on lately.
Recordings of major matches or famous contenders. Helpful for those who want to learn from them.
Ephor Augusta's are the most popular. Four years ago, she became the champion and a household name by crushing every single opponent. From there, she worked her way up into the Ephor's seat.
But I prefer watching Contender Mya's matches over any of the past champions.
I think she's... got real heart.
You can feel it, even through the recordings. The way she threw herself into every fight.
Ha, not likely. She disappeared during a Great Agon years ago.
Some say she got tangled up in illegal betting scandals. Others think the pressure from her manager drove her to leave Septimont... There are plenty of rumors, but no one knows what really happened.
Still, it's frustrating! To just disappear halfway through...
If you fail to make it to the end, people will see you as nothing.
As expected, the Silvas chose to face us in the semi-finals. The news spreads quickly.
Of course. To them, it's a provocation... and to forfeit would be a disgrace.
Domina Julia must be here. She wouldn't miss a moment like this.
She's shrewd and exceptionally capable but arrogant to the core. She's convinced that she's always right.
She doesn't believe in talking things through, thinks it's for the weak. So we'll show her we don't need rigged matches to win. We'll win with pure stength!
Come, my fellow warrior! Victory is ours for the taking!
Esteemed Ephor, I must bring a grave matter to your attention.
In this very match, the Silvas stooped to underhanded tactics!
This spear… it was a gift from Domina Julia, head of House Silva, when I first joined the family.
Back then, I trusted the Silvas completely, never once doubting their intentions. That is, until I chose to leave them not long ago.
Now in today's arena, this spear spiraled out of control, nearly stabbing my own teammate!
That's Iuno, the <te href=850152>Priestess</te> of Septimont. She rarely leaves the Temple and only shows up accompanying Ephor Augusta.
The Priestess can foretell fate and has the power to make final calls here. Most people trust her judgment.
But... Iuno is a bit extreme in our line of Priestesses. Not everyone agrees with her behavior, despite her capabilities.
...Heh, who's to say? How could I predict Domina Julia would tamper with my spear?
Skip to the end, Cartur. You're not here to catch up or spread gossip.
Deal. But we'll need new terms. I trust you'll draw up a contract that satisfies me, Cartur?
The Silvas lit the spark, but you turned a blind eye to what was happening. Don't fool yourself, Cartur. You're complicit in this, too.
Wait. You didn't come to me just for money, did you?
Finally, some peace. What a day!
Ephor Augusta had a humble origin. Her path to power was paved in blood.
Decades ago, corruption ran rampant in Septimont, far worse than it is now. Two Ephors met untimely ends back then.
The Great Agon carries immense weight for the city, and I guess that's why she chose this moment to act.
The Priestess of Septimont has the authority to make final judgments. Iuno, in particular, is one of Ephor Augusta's staunchest allies.
Most prophets spend their days in the Temple, observing fate's flow and tending to the eternal flames.
But Iuno's different. She prefers to spend her time in the hunting grounds, bow in hand, patrolling with the other fighters.
Born during an eclipse, she's the most gifted among her line. Impressive, isn't it?
That's right! I owe you for helping me escape Julia's tricks.
{PlayerName}!
I've finished talking with them, let's go back. Oh, almost forgot. This is for you!
The Heartpour I wanted you to try before. Chilled is the best way to have it.
Ugh, this is so exhausting! Dealing with these headaches takes more energy than fighting in the arena.
What is it? You're radiating worry.
If it's about tomorrow's final, maybe you should relax a bit.
Sure, but why the sudden interest?
Well, Contender Mya... I first heard about her from Domina Julia.
Julia didn't like her. Probably because they once competed in the same Agon. Mya disappeared after claiming the title, leaving that Agon without an official champion.
"An ignorant fool." That's how Julia described her. Back then, I'd just been "purchased" by the Silvas, and I got curious about the story.
Turns out, Mya spent almost all her time out of the arena training. Beyond the barest of needs, she devoted herself to honing her craft.
That kind of dedication wasn't... well-regarded in Septimont back then.
Rumor has it she got caught in dirty match-fixing, cracked under the pressure, and ran.
Everyone has their breaking point. If she ran because of it, I don't blame her.
Well, maybe I just handle these things better, or maybe I just don't care about people's expectations.
I'm not above the commercial events. It's how I made a name for myself, built my worth. As long as I'm in the arena, I'm happy.
Applause comes and goes. But the things that matter... facilities, resources, opportunities, they don't come cheap.
Septimont has its shadows, but some things still have to get done.
I don't mind being used, as long as whoever I work with knows where the line is.
I know it's easy to turn a blind eye to the dirty games, but that's just handing Septimont over to the vultures.
The Silvas played dirty in the arena I hold dear. They trampled over a line that should never be crossed. So we're done.
Look at it... How beautiful.
To the great City of Septimont.
{PlayerName}, look over there! That's Arsinosa!
Oh... Hold on, I forgot to tell you something yesterday about Mya.
Decades ago, during the Great Agon, Mya disappeared on her way to receive the award. But before that, she did claim the title of the Bloody Agon and even received Arsinosa's blessing.
The current two-on-two matchup was only introduced thanks to Augusta and her predecessor.
Before that, the matches were... far more brutal.
Gladiators were thrown into a chaotic free-for-all. To hype up the event, the organizers would unleash hordes of Tacet Discords into the arena. The last Gladiator standing was crowned the victor.
At least, changes have been made since then!
Refined by flames and tempests, all that endures eventually becomes part of Septimont's legacy.
Listen, {PlayerName}! Those cheers are for us! They wait for us to give them a fight to remember!
Are you ready to win this with me?
Wonderful! Now it's hunting time, my fellow warrior!
What's wrong? You seem like you're... somewhere else.
Er... we defeated our opponents?
As a Gladiator, it's a great honor to claim the title with you, {PlayerName}.
Let's savor this moment of glory and joy!
Here you are!
Well. If anything, she despises the Acolytes in Ragunna. Her obsession with titles is for the glory that comes with them.
She prides herself on being the "perfect Septimontian." It's highly unlikely she'd believe in the Sentinel.
Sorry, lend me your shoulder for a moment... I'm a bit tired.
Well, that's settled! Looks like the other two teams have wrapped up their match. The semi-finals are over. Finally, we can leave and get some rest.
Alright, alright, everyone! I'll chat with you later. But first, let's give my fellow warrior a moment to breathe, okay?
{Male=He;Female=She}'s new to Septimont. I'm sure none of you want to get carried away and leave a bad impression on an outlander, hm?
Go. Find somewhere quiet to rest. I'll meet up with you once I've dealt with all this.
You've come, Warrior.
Just wheat juice!
Ah, today you...
You smell nice. Like dried wheat. Warm.
Well.. that's just what I think! When I was little, I used to roll in the wheat fields.
My-my-my-my Forte gave me the tail, but it seems my nose is keener than most.
Haha... it's good to have {PlayerName} by my side!
Even though it's over, there's still a heaviness. A drink helps.
I've sent word to the Ephor's Palace about Domina Julia, but finding the Silvas that fled will take time.
Four years ago, Augusta won the title of champion. Since becoming Ephor, much of the corruption tied to the arena has been cleared.
If Mya had lived, perhaps things would have been different.
Sometimes I wonder... What would my life have been like if my parents hadn't handed me over to the Silvas when we first came to Septimont?
Just like one of those stories, with the different endings...
Haha... No need. I'm content with what I have.
For everything gained, something is lost. But through it all, I met you. That's enough.
Life is an arena. Victory and defeat are a part of that.
No one wins forever. The more victories, the higher the expectation, and the heavier the weight of defeat.
Do you fear that one day... you might face defeat?
...You seem to live to meet others' expectations. This is what you're used to, I'd say.
Ha, that suits you well! To move forward without the burden of a lofty vow is a lighter path.
Even if one day you meet defeat, remember this. Those you've helped will never let you face it alone.
I may not have your strength, but should you call, I will meet you on the battlefield. No matter the distance.
I swear it by the eternal sun, my fellow warrior.
You, my fellow warrior, are someone worth chasing. Yes, we've been chasing victory all along, but with you, it feels like victory itself was itching to catch up.
Next time we meet, you'll be even stronger, as will I. I'll be a Gladiator who can stand toe-to-toe with you!
You really live up to the name. Well fought, my fellow warrior!
lines.quest_name: All Out! Towards the Peaks of Prestige

lines.text: Heh, your strategies... they'd even work in real gladiating. I learned a lot today.
lines.quest_name: All Out! Towards the Peaks of Prestige

lines.text: No need for modesty. After a hard-won victory, you should bask in the applause and cheers. Too much humility only dulls the shine of glory.
lines.quest_name: All Out! Towards the Peaks of Prestige

lines.text: I bet this win added another brilliant mark to the honor you carry.
lines.quest_name: All Out! Towards the Peaks of Prestige

lines.text: Oh, by the way, I heard there's another duelist who's earned the right to challenge the Apex Duelist. A mysterious one.
lines.quest_name: All Out! Towards the Peaks of Prestige

lines.text: I don't know him, but I did catch a glimpse of him once.
lines.quest_name: All Out! Towards the Peaks of Prestige

lines.text: Even from the outline of his admittedly broad back, you can tell he's someone who's used to gambling with his life.
lines.quest_name: All Out! Towards the Peaks of Prestige

lines.text: I don't think he'll be able to topple you, but still, be careful when you face him.
lines.quest_name: All Out! Towards the Peaks of Prestige

lines.text: May victory smile upon you, {PlayerName}.
lines.quest_name: All Out! Towards the Peaks of Prestige

lines.text: Mm, it never hurts to keep an eye out. Good luck, {PlayerName}!
lines.quest_name: All Out! Towards the Peaks of Prestige

lines.text: An old acquaintance, huh? You've sure got a colorful circle, my fellow warrior! But once you're both on the battlefield, there's no holding back. After all, there'll be only one winner.
lines.quest_name: All Out! Towards the Peaks of Prestige

lines.text: Anyway, good luck out there! I look forward to seeing you back at it again, {PlayerName}!
lines.quest_name: All Out! Towards the Peaks of Prestige
